project.name: Family Meal Planner
project.idea: A practical meal planning app for busy families with picky eaters, shared grocery lists, budget-aware recipes, and substitution suggestions.

messages.text: I want a family meal planning app. The big problem is that everyone has different dislikes, allergies, and schedules.
Let's lock the product shape before the team builds. I need to capture users, rules for substitutions, grocery workflows, and what success looks like.
The first version should be a web app. Parents can build a weekly plan, mark dislikes for each person, and get a grocery list.
Good. I will produce the founding docs the agents need: product brief, technical spec, data model, roadmap, and acceptance criteria.

artifacts.title: Product brief
artifacts.content:
# Family Meal Planner - Product Brief

## Audience
Busy parents who already know that AI can generate recipes but need a real, owned app that respects family preferences, schedules, allergies, and budget.

## Core promise
Turn a messy set of family constraints into a weekly plan, then produce a grocery list that can actually be used at the store.

## MVP outcomes
- Create household members with dislikes, favorites, dietary restrictions, and allergy flags.
- Generate or manually assemble a seven day meal plan.
- Swap meals without breaking restrictions.
- Produce a consolidated grocery list grouped by store section.
- Explain substitutions in plain language so the family trusts the plan.

## Non-goals
- Restaurant delivery.
- Calorie tracking.
- Social feed features.

artifacts.title: Technical spec
artifacts.content:
# Technical Spec

## Stack
- React + TypeScript for the frontend.
- Node API routes for planning and grocery-list generation.
- Prisma with Postgres for households, recipes, constraints, and plans.

## System modules
1. Household profile manager.
2. Recipe catalog and tagging.
3. Constraint engine for allergies, dislikes, and schedules.
4. Meal plan board.
5. Grocery list compiler.
6. Review and drift checks against this Foundry spec.

## Quality bar
Every generated plan must preserve allergy constraints. Dislikes can be overridden, but allergies cannot.

artifacts.title: Prisma data model
artifacts.content:
# Prisma Schema Draft

```prisma
model Household {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  name      String
  members   Member[]
  recipes   Recipe[]
  mealPlans MealPlan[]
}

model Member {
  id          String @id @default(cuid())
  householdId String
  name        String
  dislikes    String[]
  allergies   String[]
  favorites   String[]
}

model Recipe {
  id          String @id @default(cuid())
  householdId String
  title       String
  ingredients Json
  tags        String[]
  prepMinutes Int
}

model MealPlan {
  id          String @id @default(cuid())
  householdId String
  weekStart   DateTime
  meals       MealPlanItem[]
}
```

artifacts.title: Build roadmap
artifacts.content:
# Build Roadmap

## Slice 1 - Data foundation
Create household, member, recipe, and meal plan models with seed data.

## Slice 2 - Planning UI
Build a weekly board that shows breakfast, lunch, and dinner slots.

## Slice 3 - Preference engine
Apply hard allergy rules and soft dislike rules to recipe selection.

## Slice 4 - Grocery list
Compile ingredients across the week, group by store section, and allow checking off items.

## Slice 5 - Drift review
Compare implementation against the founding docs before merge.

artifacts.title: Acceptance criteria
artifacts.content:
# Acceptance Criteria

- A parent can create at least three household members.
- Each member can store dislikes and allergies.
- The weekly board can hold at least 21 meal slots.
- The planner never assigns a recipe containing a member allergy.
- Grocery output groups ingredients by produce, dairy, meat, pantry, and frozen.
- Review notes show whether changed files align with the Foundry docs.

members.summary: Coordinating Foundry docs, task delegation, and merge readiness.
Building the data foundation and meal planning board.
Checking constraints, test coverage, and validation output.
Reviewing implementation against the founding docs and drift report.

tasks.subject: Create household and recipe data model
tasks.description: Add the first Prisma-style schema draft and repository seed objects for households, members, recipes, and meal plans.
acceptanceCriteria: Allergy fields are modeled separately from dislikes.

tasks.subject: Build preference questionnaire
tasks.description: Create the onboarding flow that asks for allergies, dislikes, favorite meals, budget, and cooking schedule.
tasks.reviewSummary: Adds the first questionnaire surface and stores allergies separately from dislikes.

tasks.subject: Add substitution rules and validation tests
tasks.description: Verify that allergy rules are hard blocks while dislikes produce warnings and substitution suggestions.

tasks.subject: Compile grocery list from weekly plan
tasks.description: Group ingredients by store section and dedupe repeated pantry items.

tasks.subject: Review drift against Foundry docs
tasks.description: Confirm the team is still building the product described by the founding documents.

lead.text: I have the Foundry docs loaded. I am splitting the app into data, questionnaire, testing, and grocery-list work.

input.text: Start with the household and preference model. Keep allergy rules hard-blocked.

lead.text: Developer has the first implementation task, tester is watching allergy coverage, and reviewer is tracking drift.

developer.text: I am adding the household model and preference questionnaire. The data model follows the Prisma draft from Foundry.

input.text: Implemented household, member, and recipe seed shapes. Allergies are separate from dislikes.

developer.text: The first review package is ready for the preference questionnaire. I kept files inside the planned scope.

tester.text: I am checking that the substitution engine refuses allergy conflicts and only warns for dislikes.
Validation is passing in the demo run. Next I want coverage for grocery section grouping.

reviewer.text: I am comparing the changed questionnaire files against the product brief and acceptance criteria.

input.summary: Questionnaire matches the Foundry docs and keeps allergy handling visible.

reviewer.text: Scope drift is clean for this review. The app is still aligned with the founding docs.
